experiments.description: WebXR immersive-AR session with tap-to-place anchors relative to session origin. Demonstrates browser-native local tracking with honest drift labelling.
experiments.expectedAccuracy: Room-scale only — resets on session restart
experiments.driftNote: Session origin drifts over time. Objects will not remain in place across sessions.
limitations: No GPS — cannot place objects at real-world coordinates
High drift over time without visual re-localisation
experiments.bestUseCase: Testing basic AR anchor placement; indoor demos; device capability validation

experiments.description: Leaflet.js map showing live GPS position, accuracy circle, and user-placed POI markers. Includes a compass-bearing panel and optional camera overlay mode.
experiments.driftNote: GPS signal can degrade in urban canyons or indoors.
limitations: Map/navigation mode only — objects not placed in 3D AR space
experiments.bestUseCase: Outdoor POI visualisation; GPS accuracy assessment; map + bearing UI prototype

experiments.description: Real-time GPS journey tracker with live speed, distance, max speed, heading compass and a colour-coded path canvas. Speed-gradient colourmap (green→amber→red). Pinch/pan canvas map. No external dependencies — pure canvas + Geolocation API.
experiments.driftNote: GPS accuracy varies by environment; speed calculation relies on fix frequency.
limitations: Speed derived from successive GPS fixes — may spike at low accuracy
experiments.bestUseCase: Live walk/run stats; speed-colour path visualisation; GPS capability assessment

experiments.description: Walk a loop and drop up to 12 GPS waypoints. After collecting at least 6 markers the app validates spacing (≥5 m), then generates a dungeon-style corridor map from your real-world path. The interior of any closed loop is shown as fog-of-war — only the walked edge is accessible.
experiments.expectedAccuracy: 5–15 m GPS — waypoints spaced ≥5 m required
experiments.driftNote: Map generation uses GPS coordinates relative to origin. Accuracy affects corridor shape.
limitations: Corridor map is approximate — GPS accuracy bounds corridor alignment
Interior fog-of-war is intentional; user can only walk along their waypoint path
Loop closure requires last waypoint within ~30 m of first
experiments.bestUseCase: GPS world generation; corridor mapping; gamified outdoor exploration

experiments.description: Record a GPS trail while walking, then watch a cinematic animated replay. The path is drawn progressively with speed-coded colours and a live speed readout. Scrub through the timeline to jump to any moment. Full stats: distance, max speed, average speed, duration.
experiments.driftNote: Recording quality depends on GPS fix frequency and accuracy.
limitations: Recording requires continuous GPS — indoor use unreliable
Replay is device-local only — no cloud storage
experiments.bestUseCase: Route review; speed analysis; walk/run replay cinema

experiments.description: Walk around and fill in a GPS grid — every cell you visit lights up, speed-coded from green (walking) to red (sprinting). Track visited area, unlock achievements at coverage milestones, and watch a live coverage donut. Configurable cell size: 5, 10, 20, or 50 m.
experiments.expectedAccuracy: Cell size determines minimum meaningful GPS accuracy (~10 m recommended)
experiments.driftNote: GPS jitter may register neighbouring cells spuriously at high accuracy settings.
limitations: Grid is session-local — no persistence across reloads
GPS jitter can mark adjacent cells at 5 m cell size
experiments.bestUseCase: Gamified outdoor exploration; territory coverage tracking; GPS accuracy demo
